# fix(kaizen): correct research/adapter.py Signature.inputs/outputs to List[str] + Tier 2 regression (#814)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `eb2c42f01662` — fix(kaizen): correct research/adapter.py Signature.inputs/outputs to List[str] + Tier 2 regression (#814)

**Body**:

research/adapter.py:119 was passing dict[str, str] to Signature(inputs=, outputs=)
which expects List[str]. The dict's keys ended up populating Signature._inputs_list
in iteration order, but the contract was silently broken — every downstream
consumer that read _inputs_list operated on dict-keys-as-list rather than the
intended param-name list. Pyright surfaced this as reportArgumentType warnings;
the underlying corruption was real and silent.

Fix: convert dict -> List[str] at the call site:

  inputs: List[str] = list(param_names) if param_names else ["input"]
  outputs: List[Union[str, List[str]]] = ["result"]

Added List, Union to typing imports.

Tier 2 behavioral regression at tests/regression/test_issue_814_research_adapter_inputs_list.py
— per rules/testing.md "Behavioral Regression Tests Over Source-Grep". Test
calls the real adapter entrypoint and asserts:

  - isinstance(sig._inputs_list, list)
  - all entries are str (not dict-stringified)
  - canonical content matches param-name list

Test loads adapter.py via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location() to bypass
the pre-existing kaizen.research.__init__.py orphan re-exports (Cluster C,
Shard 2). Once Shard 2 deletes the orphans (todo 2.1), the test simplifies to
a normal `from kaizen.research.adapter import ResearchAdapter`. Documented
workaround, not a stub.

Pyright: adapter.py 0 errors, 0 warnings post-fix. Closes Cluster D in #814.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
